snippet.title: Warning: Cisco's Soaring Stock Price Won't Last
snippet.description:
#cscopricetarget #ciscostocknews #cscostockprice

In this video, we're going to look at Cisco and their soaring stock price. However, we believe that this will not last for very long, and that the stock price will eventually drop. Let's take a look at some of the reasons why we believe this.

Cisco Systems (CSCO) reported fiscal fourth quarter results that beat analyst estimates, but the company's outlook was cautious. In an interview with Cisco Systems CEO Chuck Robbins says the the company benefited from a more "diverse book of business" than its rivals and some supply chain challenges starting to ease. On the economy, Robbins says "it's clearly mixed," with some industries, like financial services and transportation, showing strength.

When it comes to AI, Robbins says the company is getting some "early wins with some of this next generation ethernet." Robbins says the company provides the networking equipment that connect the "brains" behind the AI systems. He adds that the company will see some benefit in its 2024 fiscal year, but "we think the real move to this new technology" will be in fiscal 2025. Robbins add that he thinks "the opportunity over the next 5,6,7 years could be three times what the original cloud buildout was."

snippet.title: “5 Minute Salute” with Chuck Wald of Robbins-Gioia hosted by Hellena Pugh
snippet.description:
🫡Welcome to the "5 Minute Salute" with Hellena Pugh, where we celebrate and honor our nation's veterans. This video features an insightful interview with Chuck Wald, a distinguished Air Force veteran and successful CEO of Robbins Gioya in Washington, DC.

Chuck's journey into the military began during the Vietnam War era, where he had various opportunities, including pursuing a law degree or playing professional football for the Atlanta Falcons. However, his passion for aviation led him to flight school and a fulfilling career in the Air Force.

Today, as CEO of Robbins Gioia, Chuck oversees a cutting-edge management tool that handles scheduling and planning for thousands of aircraft and personnel. With decades of experience in the aviation and defense industry, Chuck brings valuable insights to the table.

During the interview, Chuck shares essential advice for transitioning veterans. He highlights the importance of adapting to the civilian workforce, understanding the corporate world's bottom line focus, and leveraging the GI Bill (Post 9/11) for education and skill development.

Chuck's impressive career and dedication to excellence serve as a shining example of the possibilities that await veterans after military service.
